titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert Dudley French Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 5, 2018 at 07:36

did.abstract: Correspondence, writings, research notes, teaching materials, printed matter, a diary, a scrapbook, and photographs (1922-1952) comprise the papers. The teaching materials and the research notes reflect French's interest in Chaucer. A portion of the correspondence concerns Memorial Quadrangle at Yale, about which he published a book in 1929. There is also a substantial number of letters from students at Jonathan Edwards College, of which he was master from 1930 to 1953. Other correspondents are Robert Bates, John Philip Marchant and Emerson Tuttle.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert Dudley French Papers (MS 1264). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Dwight Blakeslee, 1981; gift of Bernard Lytton, 2001.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Dudley French (1888-1954): professor of English; A.B., Yale, 1910, A.M., 1914, Ph.D., 1920; instructor in English, Yale, 1914-1919, assistant professor, 1919-1926, associate professor, 1926-1930, professor and master of Jonathan Edwards College, 1930-1953.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, writings, research notes, teaching materials, printed matter, a diary, a scrapbook, and photographs (1922-1952) comprise the papers. The teaching materials and the research notes reflect French's interest in Chaucer. A portion of the correspondence concerns Memorial Quadrangle at Yale, about which he published a book in 1929. There is also a substantial number of letters from students at Jonathan Edwards College, of which he was master from 1930 to 1953. Other correspondents are Robert Bates, John Philip Marchant and Emerson Tuttle.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Preliminary Inventory by Tina Fagas, 1981 Apr

title.part: The Diary of a Wandering Philosopher

did.unittitle: Canturbury Tales, Croft Classics Text Collection
Curtis, J.T., [ed.] The Vision of Piers Plowman
Somnocasia or The House of Sleep
Material relating to Avon Old Farms
Birth and Wedding Announcements of Jonathan Edwards Students
Chaucer: Research Report No. 14 Committee on Research and Bibliography of the Chaucer Group of the Modern Language Association of America
